name: follow-up-cleanup-pass-after-20260411-0608
idea:
Follow-up cleanup pass after the identity-based-routing megaplan. Four narrow, focused fixes to close quality gaps that the identity-routing plan explicitly left as TODO:

1. WIRE UP THE LLM CLASSIFIER that was scaffolded but never called. The identity-routing megaplan's admin_chat_cog instantiates an AsyncAnthropic client (self._classifier_client) in __init__ at line 65 and logs a warning if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing ('payment reply classification will fail closed'), but the actual classifier function _classify_confirmation is a classmethod that never touches self._classifier_client. The classifier function is keyword-based only. The operator specifically asked for LLM-based classification during scoping of the previous megaplan, and this was injected as a steering note, but the revise worker overrode it and specified keywords instead. This cleanup closes that gap: convert _classify_confirmation from a classmethod to an instance method, call self._classifier_client.messages.create with a narrow structured-output prompt (Anthropic tool_use API to force the schema) to classify the recipient's reply as confirmed/not_received/unclear, fall back to the existing keyword classification if the API key is missing or the call raises. The tests should cover both the LLM path (mocked) and the keyword fallback path. The LLM classifier prompt should be strict: only return confirmed if the recipient clearly indicates they see the test payment in their wallet, 'ok' or 'thanks' alone is NOT confirmed, 'wait let me check' is unclear not confirmed. The classifier must be stateless — no memory between calls, no channel history context beyond the single recipient message and the bot's prompt message. Use Claude Haiku (claude-haiku-4-5) or the current configured self._classifier_model. Structured output enforced via tool_use so the LLM cannot escape the schema.

2. FIX THE SILENT-ON-AMBIGUOUS UX HOLE in _handle_test_receipt_ambiguous. Today this handler silently increments an internal counter without telling the recipient anything. The recipient gets zero feedback on their first ambiguous reply, which is genuinely confusing — they don't know if their message was received, if the bot misunderstood, what format is expected, whether to try again. Fix: on the first ambiguous reply (count < 2), post a friendly clarification message to the recipient in the thread: 'I didn't quite understand — please reply with confirmed or yes once you see the test SOL in your wallet, or not received if you don't see it.' Keep the existing 2-strike escalation to negative. If the LLM classifier (from fix 1) returns unclear with a reply field, use that reply instead of a hardcoded template.

3. FIX THE NEGATIVE-PATH RECIPIENT MESSAGING GAP in _handle_test_receipt_negative. Today this handler posts a message addressed to the admin ('@admin manual review needed for intent X...') which the recipient can see but is not directed at them. The recipient has no idea what to do next. Fix: post a parallel recipient-facing message alongside the admin tag: 'Hey @recipient — I've flagged this for admin review. Please wait for @admin to follow up.' Do not remove the admin tag or the DM — keep both. Add the recipient-directed text as a separate send, so the admin still gets the alert they expect.

4. DELETE THE DEAD CODE that the identity-routing megaplan explicitly retained 'for staged cleanup in a follow-up'. The admin_chat_cog.py line 1052-1053 comment says: 'Identity-routing note: MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT, _can_user_message_bot, and _is_directed_at_bot are intentionally retained as dead code for staged cleanup.' This cleanup actually removes them. Specifically delete: _can_user_message_bot method and any imports/calls, _is_directed_at_bot method if it exists, MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant and any conditional tool palette logic that branches on admin vs approved-member. Verify nothing still references them via grep. Update any tests that exercised approved-member paths — those tests should be deleted or rewritten to test the identity router's fall-through behavior (non-admin, non-pending-recipient user should cause the cog to do nothing).

SCOPE CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL — do not exceed):
- Only touch files in src/features/admin_chat/ and their tests
- Do NOT touch src/features/grants/solana_client.py, src/features/payments/solana_provider.py, payment_service.py, payment_worker_cog.py, payment_ui_cog.py, producer_flows.py, db_handler.py, or any other payment subsystem files. The identity-routing megaplan's cross-subsystem work is DONE and must stay that way.
- Do NOT modify the state machine transitions themselves (awaiting_wallet, awaiting_test_receipt_confirmation, awaiting_admin_approval). Only modify the HANDLERS for those states to add recipient-facing messaging.
- Do NOT add new intent states, new DB columns, new tools, or new flows. Pure cleanup.
- Do NOT change the orphan sweep, the AdminApprovalView, the query tools, the batch tool, upsert_wallet_for_user, or any other feature from the identity-routing megaplan.
- Preserve all hardening invariants: fail-closed on classifier failure, admin DM gate for real payments, auto-skip for verified wallets, test-first for unverified wallets, etc.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- self._classifier_client is actually used by _classify_confirmation (no longer dead code)
- LLM classifier path works end-to-end with structured output via tool_use
- Keyword classifier path is preserved as the fallback for when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is unset or the API call raises
- First ambiguous reply from recipient receives a clear in-thread message asking them to clarify
- Negative-path escalation posts both an admin-facing message (existing) AND a recipient-facing message (new)
- MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT, _can_user_message_bot, _is_directed_at_bot are fully deleted; grep finds no references
- All existing tests still pass (189 baseline)
- New tests cover: LLM classifier happy path (confirmed/not_received/unclear), keyword fallback when API key missing, keyword fallback when API call raises, recipient clarification message on first ambiguous, recipient-facing message on negative path, dead code removal verification

DELIBERATELY OUT OF SCOPE:
- Changing the architecture (identity router stays, state machine stays)
- Multi-admin support
- Reply-to-bot detection (currently any in-thread message triggers the state machine; this is not being changed)
- Grants cog
- Any cross-subsystem refactoring
- New tools or features
- Manual smoke tests (those are a separate checklist outside any megaplan)

history.message: Claude step failed: Claude returned an error
